Bug 1969432 - Treat table captions as paragraph boundaries. r=masayuki

They create a block even tho they're not block-outside in the CSS sense.
This makes captions consistent with table cells too.

Differential Revision: https://phabricator.services.mozilla.com/D251997

Bug 1968773 [wpt PR 52812] - Modify Cookie Store API WPTs cleanup methods,

Automatic update from web-platform-tests
Modify Cookie Store API WPTs cleanup methods

This adds explicit cleanups for cookies set using the HTTP `Set-Cookie`
header that cannot be deleted otherwise. HTTP-only cookies are not
allowed to be written to by the Cookie Store API. BOM characters are
supported in CookieStore read operations but not writes so cookies that
contain them cannot be cleaned up with this API.

Change-Id: Ibb86a9b9dbf857d6c95276190d899db12947d561

Bug 1968793 [wpt PR 52815] - [animation-trigger] Repeat triggers should not cancel the animation,

Automatic update from web-platform-tests
[animation-trigger] Repeat triggers should not cancel the animation

Currently, repeat triggers cancel their animations. This isn't the
right behavior. Repeat triggers ought to reset their animations to the
effect's before phase.

This patch is a step in that right direction which, instead of
cancelling the animation, sets the current time of the animation and
pauses it. This patch partially introduces the notion of
`pause_for_trigger_` which is a necessary bit to signify that the
animation is paused in anticipation of a trigger event. A future patch
will implement the pause_for_trigger_ semantics more fully by factoring
it into decisions about the effect's timing & phase.

animation-trigger-repeat-tentative.html now reflects that the animation
is not canceled but simply reset to its initial state.

Bug 1898654 - Make EXPANDABLE/EXPANDED/COLLAPSED states consistent. r=Jamie

Calculate COLLAPSED as a presence of EXPANDABLE with an absence of
EXPANDED. Also, tried to remove direct uses of COLLAPSED whereever
reasonable.

Note: We will still fire COLLAPSED state changes, so that's good.

Differential Revision: https://phabricator.services.mozilla.com/D251745

Bug 1859474 - Don't release TCPSocket on socket thread. r=necko-reviewers,kershaw

Since TCPSocket might be released on the socket thread, it might cause a
crash when TCPSocket is destroyed on this thread.

Since we use mozilla::SyncRunnable::DispatchToThread, this is synchronous
call. So it is unnecessary to touch reference count on this thread.

Differential Revision: https://phabricator.services.mozilla.com/D251990

Bug 1968737 [wpt PR 52807] - Replace contentEncoding value in resourceTiming "unknown" with "@unknown",

Automatic update from web-platform-tests
Replace contentEncoding value in resourceTiming "unknown" with "@unknown"

Per discussion in ietf and webperf WG folks, we don't reserve "unknown" value for the http parameter "content coding". Instead, we use a different value "@unknown" that cannot be registered as value of "content coding", to present a filtered value. The "@unknown" cannot be registered because it contains '@' that's prohibited.
